name: Neutron lifetime anomaly (beam vs bottle)
summary: Two methods of measuring the free-neutron lifetime give discrepant results at ~4 sigma: 'bottle' experiments confine ultracold neutrons and count survivors (tau_bottle ~ 877.75 +/- 0.28 s), while 'beam' experiments measure decay-product protons in a cold-neutron beam (tau_beam ~ 887.7 +/- 2.2 s). The 10 s gap could indicate either a systematic in one method (most likely beam, due to undercounted final-state protons), an exotic decay channel n -> dark + gamma at the few-permil level, or new physics in beta-decay matrix elements.

characteristic_numbers.note: Latest precision UCN-bottle measurement (Gonzalez et al. 2021).
Discrepancy at ~4 sigma; if bottle is correct and beam is missing protons, exotic channel BR ~ 1% at Delta E ~ 1 MeV.

principles_stressed.note: If interpreted as beta-decay BR shift, would imply new V-A or scalar/tensor currents at 10^-3 level - but other measurements (cabibbo unitarity, R(D)) bound such couplings tightly. More plausibly a systematic; also interesting as a constraint on dark-decay channels n -> chi + photon.

history: Discrepancy emerged in the 2010s as bottle experiments became more precise. Czarnecki, Marciano, Sirlin (2018, arXiv:1802.01804) systematic review. Fornal-Grinstein (2018, PRL 120, 191801) proposed n -> chi gamma exotic channel. Active experimental program: UCNtau (LANL), magneto-gravitational traps, cold-neutron beam at NIST.

references.citation: Czarnecki, Marciano, Sirlin, 'Neutron lifetime and axial coupling connection', PRL 120, 202002 (2018)
Fornal and Grinstein, 'Dark matter interpretation of the neutron decay anomaly', Phys. Rev. Lett. 120, 191801 (2018)
